sourceTitle: Tinkercad Tutorial - How To Make An Advanced 3D Cat

tutorialSteps.title: Head
tutorialSteps.note: Start with a sphere, scaled to about 28.26 x 24.96 x 24.66 mm.

tutorialSteps.title: Ears
tutorialSteps.note: Use mirrored paraboloids for pointed ears, plus smaller hole-like inserts to hollow the ear silhouette.

tutorialSteps.title: Mouth and nose bridge
tutorialSteps.note: Build the muzzle from overlapping spheres, then use a rounded roof/roof primitive for the bridge and nose.

tutorialSteps.title: Head shaping and eyes
tutorialSteps.note: Use hole spheres to carve eye and cheek spaces, then add mirrored eye spheres and smaller pupils.

tutorialSteps.title: Body and neck
tutorialSteps.note: Add a long sphere for the body, about 50 x 25 mm, tilt it slightly, then connect it with a tilted cylinder neck.

tutorialSteps.title: Front legs
tutorialSteps.note: Use spheres for shoulders, cylinders for upper/lower leg sections, and half-sphere paws.

tutorialSteps.title: Back legs
tutorialSteps.note: Add longer rotated spheres for back haunches and repeat the leg/paw pattern on both sides.

tutorialSteps.title: Tail
tutorialSteps.note: Use a torus, cut it with a box in Tinkercad, and stand the remaining arc up behind the body.

primitivePlan: mirrored after one ear is shaped
two upper lobes plus one lower lobe
bridge 7 x 2 mm, nose 5 x 2 mm
eyes about 7.27 x 8.23 x 7 mm, pupils 3 x 3 x 4 mm
shoulders 13 x 14 x 11 mm, legs 8 mm dia
40 mm torus, 9 mm thick in tutorial
box-cut torus represented as a mesh arc
